Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Complaint For Failure To Serve Defendant Within Three Years Of Filing Complaint Ccp 583 250
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Morgan, Esq. 866-205-4971 of Hogan Injury for Plaintiff.
Paul Yee, Esq. 510-457-9923 of the Law Office of Lori R. Mayfield for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his views re: service. Defendant argues and cites Thompson case. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT BONNIE PAULSEN STREET AS TRUSTEE OF THE MARITAL, RICHARD M PAULSEN AS TRUSTEE OF THE MARITAL TRUST, ROYCE T PAULSEN AS TRUSTEE OF THE MARITAL TRUST, JOHN H PAULSEN AS TRUSTEE OF THE MARITAL TRUST, ALICE PAULSEN, THE TESTATE AND INTESTATE's Motion To Dismiss Complaint For Failure To Serve Defendant Within Three Years Of Filing Complaint Ccp 583 250.

The Paulsen Defendants' motion to dismiss complaint for failure to serve within three years of filing complaint is denied as to John Paulsen as trustee of the marital trust and granted as to all other moving defendants. As calculated by plaintiff Steven Julia, the length of any tolling due to the impossibility of serving the Paulsen Defendants was seven months, while service on all of them other than John Paulsen was nine months after the three year period expired. Since none of the Paulsen Defendants other than John Paulsen were validly served within the three year period as extended by the tolling period of seven months, all of the Paulsen defendants other than John Paulsen are dismissed from this case. While it is an unusual application of CCP 473(a), the court will permit Mr. Julia to amend his complaint to substitute "John Paulsen as Trustee of the Marital Trust created under the Albert and Alice Paulsen 1986 Family Trust" in place of "Alice Hardy Paulsen" (sued as Doe Two). Under the unusual circumstances of this case, permitting such an amendment comports with the strong California policy of deciding cases on their merits and is not inconsistent with the policy underlying CCP 583.210 because Mr. Paulsen received service within the three year period extended by the tolling period. Mr. Julia must file his amendment no later than December 29, 2017. The parties should meet and confer regarding a continuance of the trial date. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR# 6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Patrick Hogan of Hogan Injury telephone number 866-205-4971 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Steven Julia.
Michael Tcheng of Law Offices of Santana, Tcheng, Vierra & Symonds telephone number 415-276-0705 for all Defendants.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling and overruled the objection as to Mr. Hogan's Client's declaration. DEFENDANTS MUSA DAJANI, MUSA S. DAJANI TRUST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. The moving papers were timely filed and served and the motion is arguably based on new evidence. There is a triable issue whether the conditions on the sidewalk contributed to plaintiff's injuries. While plaintiff testified at his deposition that the accident occurred in the street, he did not admit that the sidewalk-which is defendants' responsibility per San Francisco Public Works Code 706-had no role in the accident. Paragraphs 6-8 of plaintiff's declaration explain how the defective sidewalk contributed to the accident. The fact that the injury ultimately took place in the street is not dispositive. In Barnes v. Black (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 1473 the court found that a property owner could be liable where its steep driveway funneled a child riding a tricycle into the public street where the accident occurred. In this case, there is a triable issue whether the condition of the sidewalk similarly was a contributing factor to the accident. Mr. Hogan is to provide a form of order that includes the Court's ruling on the objection.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
